related_questions.question: What is the difference between frontlit and backlit signage?
related_questions.snippet: Front-lit channel letters are a popular choice for outdoor visibility, especially at nighttime, as their illuminated faces enhance business signage readability from a distance. Conversely, backlit signage provides a softer glow, offering unique benefits in elegance.

related_questions.question: What is the difference between front lit and halo lit?
related_questions.snippet: Halo lit channel letters (also known as halo lit or reverse channel letters) are the converse of their front lit counterparts. Instead of a translucent front side, these letters are open on the back, and positioned a little bit away from the wall.
related_questions.title: Benefits of Front and Halo Lit Channel Letter Signs (Dual Lit)

related_questions.question: What is the difference between sign and signage?
related_questions.snippet: Signs and signage are essential tools for conveying information to people, but they have different purposes. Signs are typically used to give directions or warn of danger, while signage is mainly used for advertising or marketing purposes.
